pattern: A run-length stack collapses each block of k opens immediately followed by k closes in one pass, letting newly adjacent runs merge so cascading removals fall out naturally.
hints: After a removal, the characters on either side become adjacent and may form a new removable block, so a fresh left-to-right rescan would be wasteful.
Keep a stack of (character, run-length) groups; a removal happens exactly when a group of k closing brackets sits directly on a group of k opening brackets.

Push characters, coalescing equal neighbours into run lengths. Whenever the top group is k ')' over a group of k '(', pop both and merge the groups newly exposed; the answer is the surviving runs concatenated.

edge_inputs.description: fully cancels with k=1 in two cascading steps
one unmatched pair removed, stray brackets remain
one k=3 block removed leaving balanced k=1 pairs that are not k-balanced
k=2 needs exactly two-and-two, single pairs do not qualify
nesting cascades from the inside out
removal exposes a new k-balanced block
k equals half the length, whole string vanishes

stress.description: random bracket strings with small k so removals cascade often
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('()') for _ in range(n))
    k = rng.randrange(1, max(2, n // 2 + 1))
    return [s, k]
stress.note: k bounded by n/2 per the constraint

stress.description: balanced nested strings that fully or partially collapse
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    depth = max(1, size // 2)
    s = '(' * depth + ')' * depth
    k = rng.randrange(1, depth + 1)
    return [s, k]
stress.note: deep single nest stresses the run-length merging
